name: Public invoice on iOS Safari shows Apple Pay button with correct amount
description: Customer opens the deposit-invoice link from email on her iPhone. iOS Safari + Stripe Payment Request API surfaces an Apple Pay button. The button charge amount must match the invoice's cents-correct value; tapping it presents Touch/Face ID; success returns to the page with a paid state.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.role: iPhone user, Apple Pay set up
persona.context: Tapped the email CTA from Mail; landed on the public invoice.

preconditions: Stripe Payment Request configured with Apple Pay merchant ID
Invoice in 'unpaid' status, amount in cents-correct (post-migration)
User-Agent reflects iOS Safari (or Chrome iOS — same UIWebKit)

steps.expect: Page detects PaymentRequest API support; Apple Pay button mounts above the manual card form
Apple Pay sheet is configured with displayItems totaling the invoice (cents-correct, e.g. 10750 = $107.50)

steps.action: Taps Apple Pay → confirms with Face ID
steps.expect: Stripe charges via Payment Request; on success, Stripe webhook updates Invoice → paid; client shows confirmation state
Invoice now reads 'Paid · receipt sent'; no Apple Pay button (already paid)

evaluationCriteria.check: Apple Pay button uses the canonical 'rounded' Apple-supplied style; not a custom-painted impostor
Tap-to-pay flow is two taps (CTA → biometric); no unnecessary in-page form
Cents-correct amount; Stripe idempotency keys in place; refunds flow back to the same invoice
Apple Pay button has accessible label 'Pay with Apple Pay'; the page also offers a manual-card fallback for users without Apple Pay
